title: Working Papers
text:
Master Thesis
Une dette écologique pour fonder les politiques de préservation du vivant Defining a national accounting framework in strong sustainability adapted to the fight against land artificialisation. Morgane Gonon — Université Paris Dauphine, 2021 — Veblen Prize (Master’s Thesis Award, 2022) [Full text]

title: Research
text: My research examines how biodiversity financing mechanisms shape the content and distribution of ecological investment. I focus on the contextual adequacy of instruments across income regimes and governance structures — from official development finance and voluntary carbon markets to sovereign debt and national budget reform.

Master Thesis
Une dette écologique pour fonder les politiques de préservation du vivant Defining a national accounting framework in strong sustainability adapted to the fight against land artificialisation. Université Paris Dauphine, 2021 — Veblen Prize (Master’s Thesis Award, 2022) [Full text]

title: Morgane Gonon
text:
Welcome! I am a PhD candidate in Economics at CIRED and AgroParisTech, Université Paris-Saclay.
I examine the political economy of environmental finance instruments, macro-financial constraints on sustainability transitions, and the institutional economics of North–South environmental funding. Empirical work spans Senegal, Colombia, and France, and methods include macroeconomic modelling, econometrics, and mixed-methods empirical research.
I am an Associate Researcher at the French Development Agency (AFD) and a Fellow of the Earth System Governance Research Network.
Contact: morgane.gonon [at] agroparistech.fr
